data.title: Sika's Efficiency Program is Sensible — Market Talk
data.description: Sika's 'fast forward' program is strategically sensible, Vontobel's Alexander Koller says in a note. The Swiss chemicals company's program monetizes synergies generated from the acquisition of MBCC Group, tackles structural issues in China, and hardwires digital and efficiency gains into the model, Koller says. The execution risk around restructuring is also non-trivial, he adds. The program strengthens confidence in Sika's path to reach an Ebitda margin target of more than 20%, and supports its quality growth equity story, he says. Shares trade 0.4% lower at 157.4 Swiss francs. (nina.kienle@wsj.com)

data.title: Bouvet ASA - Mandatory Notification of Trade
data.description:
Member of the Board of Bouvet ASA, Egil Dahl, on 27 November 2025, through hisinvestment company Velven Kapital, sold 400,000 shares in Bouvet ASA at apriceof NOK 60.1500 per share. Following this transaction, Egil Dahl holds1,013,020shares in Bouvet ASA.

Please see the attachment for more information.

For further information, please contact:Steffen Garder, CFO | Tel: +47 930 99 940 | Email: steffen.garder@bouvet.no

This information is subject to the disclosure requirements pursuant to theMarket Abuse Regulation (EU) No. 596/2014 Article 19 and the NorwegianSecurities Trading Act Section 5-12.

https://newsweb.oslobors.no/message/660727
